section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing. Annex templates govern post-award implementation reporting; application form templates (Tier 2A) govern proposal writing.
section13_checks.check_notes: No reference to 'Annex [0-9]', 'Grant Agreement Annex', 'AGA Template', or 'Model Grant Agreement template' appears in sub_sections[].content or traceability_footer. Structural sources in traceability_footer reference docs/tier2a_instrument_schemas/extracted/section_schema_registry.json and evaluator_expectation_registry.json — both are correct Tier 2A application form schema sources, not GA Annex templates.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents.
section13_checks.check_notes: Three call-requirement assertions are made in content: (1) 'Apply AI sectors mandated by the call (SR-07)' mapped to CS-13 (confirmed, source_ref: Tier 2: scope_requirements.json SR-07); (2) 'AI-on-demand platform integration per SR-10 and CC-05' mapped to CS-12 (confirmed, source_ref: Tier 2: scope_requirements.json SR-10; call_constraints.json CC-05); (3) 'TEF coordination per CC-07' mapped to CS-14 (confirmed, source_ref: Tier 2: call_constraints.json CC-07). traceability_footer.primary_sources includes docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/scope_requirements.json and docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json at tier 2. All call-requirement claims carry Tier 2B source references within docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, equipment — not present in Tier 3.
section13_checks.check_notes: B.3.2 names all 8 consortium partners with organisational descriptions and WP-level capabilities: ATU, BIIS, CERIA, NIHS, ISIA, ELI, BAL, FIIT. CS-11 covers all partner descriptions (confirmed, source_ref: Tier 3: consortium/partners.json partners[].description). CS-01 and CS-02 cover WP lead assignments (confirmed, source_ref: Tier 4: wp_structure.json, which itself traces to Tier 3 roles). traceability_footer.primary_sources includes docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/partners.json and docs/tier3_project_instantiation/consortium/roles.json at tier 3. No partner name, capability, or role claim lacks a Tier 3 source reference path in the traceability footer.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed. Absent budget artifacts are a blocking gate failure; no Phase 8 substep may begin until gate_09 is satisfied.
section13_checks.check_notes: This artifact carries schema_id 'orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1' — a Phase 8 artifact. CS-07 (status: confirmed, source_ref: 'Tier 4: budget_gate_assessment.json gate_pass_declaration=pass') explicitly asserts the budget gate passed. traceability_footer.primary_sources includes docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase7_budget_gate/budget_gate_assessment.json at tier 4. TAPM input boundary precludes independent disk-level verification of that file; the artifact's own confirmed CS-07 claim and traceability footer reference constitute the available evidence of gate passage. Content scan: no €-amounts, EUR amounts, or person-month cost figures appear in B.3.1 or B.3.2; all 'M[0-9]+' occurrences are project timeline month designators, not budget values. No budget-dependent content identified without a confirmed gate-pass reference.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4.
section13_checks.check_notes: Not directly checkable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs agent runtime behaviour at decision time, not artifact structure. Per skill specification, check_status is recorded as pass; enforcement is the responsibility of the decision-log-update skill. Non-applicable to artifact-level audit.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic.
section13_checks.check_notes: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition governs workflow and skill scope creep at system design level, not deliverable content. Per skill specification, check_status is recorded as pass. Non-applicable to artifact-level audit.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete.
section13_checks.check_notes: traceability_footer.primary_sources references only upstream phases relative to Phase 8: Phase 3 (wp_structure.json), Phase 4 (gantt.json), Phase 6 (implementation_architecture.json), Phase 7 (budget_gate_assessment.json), plus Tier 2 and Tier 3 sources. No forward references to same-level or downstream phase outputs are present as inputs. No indicators of phase reordering or gate weakening are detectable in the artifact structure or claim_statuses.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable.
section13_checks.check_notes: validation_status.overall_status is 'inferred', not 'unresolved'. Examined all 15 claim_statuses: CS-01 through CS-14 are 'confirmed'; CS-15 is 'inferred' with an explicit inference chain stated ('Inferred: ethics_assessment ETHICS-02 health data→SEN; SR-10/CC-05 open science mandate→PU'). No claim_statuses entry carries status 'unresolved'. The threshold for a §13.8 finding — overall_status of 'unresolved' without explicit gap flagging — is not met.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible.
section13_checks.check_notes: Content was scanned for claims asserting participation conditions, grant agreement terms, or financial regulation rules that would require Tier 1 sourcing. The reference to 'three ethics categories are triggered — Category 8 (AI), Category 4 (health data), Category 2 (human participants in medical context)' derives from the ethics self-assessment application form structure (Tier 2A), not from primary legislation or financial regulation; CS-10 traces this to Tier 4: implementation_architecture.json ethics_assessment.issues, and traceability_footer.primary_sources includes Tier 2A paths (section_schema_registry.json, evaluator_expectation_registry.json). The statement 'Administrative eligibility declarations are handled outside this B.3.2 narrative' explicitly defers eligibility claims, avoiding untraced regulatory assertions. No direct claim about participation conditions, grant agreement terms, or financial regulation lacking a Tier 1 path in the traceability footer was identified.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4.
section13_checks.check_notes: traceability_footer.no_unsupported_claims_declaration is true. All 15 claim_statuses have source_refs: CS-01 through CS-14 are confirmed with named Tier 2, Tier 3, or Tier 4 source references; CS-15 is inferred with an explicit inference chain. No claim_statuses entry has status 'unresolved' and no source_ref. traceability_footer.primary_sources spans Tier 2 (scope_requirements.json, call_constraints.json, section_schema_registry.json, evaluator_expectation_registry.json), Tier 3 (partners.json, roles.json), and Tier 4 (wp_structure.json, gantt.json, implementation_architecture.json, budget_gate_assessment.json). Traceability coverage is present for all material claims in the artifact.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions.
section13_checks.check_notes: Not checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition governs write operations to Tier 1 and Tier 2 source document files, which cannot be detected by auditing a Tier 5 output artifact. Per skill specification, check_status is recorded as pass. Non-applicable to artifact-level audit.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional.
section13_checks.check_notes: Not directly checkable from a deliverable artifact. This prohibition governs agent constitutional compliance posture, not deliverable content. Per skill specification, check_status is recorded as pass. Non-applicable to artifact-level audit.
